verdict: mixed / wash — improvements offset by real regressions; revisit before merging

dimensions.title: Correctness & numerical fidelity
dimensions.reason: logic matches under x64 but ships float32 → drift 1.7e-04 (1e-8,1e-3] → 3
dimensions.citations: results/equivalence.json: float32 run worst=1.7e-4 (ex2); x64 run worst=1.4e-14

dimensions.title: Computational efficiency (as used)
dimensions.citations: as_used_total.py: numpy 0.035s / jax 1.56s = 0.022x; fixable by not making s0_idx/T static

citations.pure_no_order_dependence: single pure compute_rc_model; original needed 3 ordered stateful methods
citations.no_global_state: removes original's reliance on module-level P,n,K
citations.good_algorithmic_choices: FALSE: pricing kernel is an O(n^2) nested fori_loop scatter (ge_arrow.md:938-959) instead of a vectorised outer product
citations.fixes_prior_bugs: fixes value_functionss typo and de-duplicates R=sum(Q)

citations.vectorised_where_natural: FALSE: kernel written as scalar loops
citations.correct_control_flow_primitive: FALSE: fori_loop used where broadcasting fits
citations.no_anti_idiomatic_constructs: FALSE: jax.lax.cond(T==0,...) traces both branches though T is static
citations.clean_call_sites_and_naming: TRUE: NamedTuple result, m.Q/m.alpha/... read cleanly

dimensions.title: API ergonomics & reusability
dimensions.citations: results/static_metrics.json: compute_rc_model(...) returns one immutable NamedTuple
